cms-1: Turn Your Worst Security Vulnerabilities into Your Strongest Defense
cms-2: Boring compliance videos don't stop hacker exploits. NINJIO delivers micro-learning episodes engineered by Hollywood storytellers to transform employee behavior, slash your Click-Through Rate, and neutralize human risk at the perimeter.
cms-6: Hackers impersonate executives using targeted psychological profiles. NINJIO trains employees to slow down, spot social engineering patterns, and systematically verify anomaly requests before clicking links.
cms-8: Fake login pages steal Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and corporate identities. We map our educational content out to show exactly how attackers clone production portals to capture active MFA tokens.
cms-10: Exploiting urgency, fear, and institutional trust to manipulate users. Micro-episodes explain the mechanics of executive impersonation, urgent payroll redirects, and supplier invoice fraud.
cms-12: Unwitting execution of malicious macros and payload attachments hidden in files. Training stops the trigger finger, building an active organizational habit of vetting unexpected attachments.
cms-14: Written by professional creators. High-stakes narratives make security concepts completely unforgettable to the end user.
cms-16: Real-time simulation engine that feeds active threat intelligence into personalized employee test vectors.
cms-18: Teaches core tactical focus rules so teams immediately recognize anomalous behavior regardless of technological medium.
cms-20: Compelling points, internal tracking leaderboards, and immediate rewards drive positive adoption without shaming users.
cms-22: New anime-style threat scripts deployed monthly based on real corporate security breaches reported globally.
cms-24: Track your precise human risk trends over time and target automated training remediations exactly where needed.
cms-25: Traditional enterprise modules waste hours of operating focus. NINJIO targets lightning-fast, high-impact intervals that shift behavioral trends without exhausting employee time.
cms-26: Unified automated management. Integrates deeply across major identity providers and active directory portals.
